PROJECT DESCRIPTION: INDUSTRIAL CAMPUS ENERGY UPGRADE – PHASE 1 COMPLETE

Project type: Energy efficiency and partial renewable integration

IMPLEMENTATION STATUS:
Phase 1 of the renewable energy system is complete. Ten solar panels have
been partially installed on the administrative building rooftop. The remaining
90 panels are awaiting import clearance.

Energy efficiency measures are partially implemented. Insulation upgrades
were completed in Building A, but Buildings B through F remain unmodified.
Overall energy reduction so far is approximately 6% against baseline.

GHG monitoring is partially commissioned. One of three planned emission
sensors is operational; the other two are partially installed and pending
calibration.

Environmental infrastructure: dust filters are partially deployed in
production hall 1, but hall 2 and hall 3 have no filtration systems yet.

Environmental certification: the LEED pre-assessment was submitted in
Q1, but the full certification audit is pending completion of remaining
installation works.

SUMMARY:
Only Phase 1 has been completed. Full commissioning of all systems
is expected once Phase 2 funding is secured. Current operational capacity
represents approximately 20% of the planned final scope.
